report_name: Hoya Corporation Disrupted by Hunters International Ransomware; $10M Ransom Demanded

threat_actors.name: Hunters International
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that executed a double-extortion attack against Hoya Corporation in April 2024, disrupting production and demanding a $10 million ransom. The group is believed to be a rebrand of the Hive ransomware operation and operates a Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) model.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through data encryption and the threat of leaking stolen data.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Hunters International deployed their ransomware to encrypt files on Hoya Corporation's systems, which was the core of the attack and led to the disruption of production and order systems.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: The Hunters International group claimed to have exfiltrated 2 terabytes of data (1.7 million files) from Hoya Corporation's network before deploying the ransomware, as part of their double-extortion tactic.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors likely attempted to prevent easy recovery from the ransomware attack by deleting volume shadow copies using commands such as 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows'.

malware.name: Hunters International Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware used by the Hunters International group to encrypt Hoya Corporation's files. The attack involved a double-extortion model, where data was also exfiltrated with a threat to leak it.

identities.name: Hoya Corporation
identities.description: A major Japanese manufacturer of optical products and medical equipment that was the victim of a ransomware attack by Hunters International in April 2024. The attack disrupted production and order systems, and the attackers demanded a $10 million ransom.

indicators.name: Hunters International Volume Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b9f3c1a-8d7e-4f6b-9c1d-5a3b2c1d8e4f
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common TTP for ransomware groups like Hunters International to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.securityandcompliance.com/news/major-cyber-attacks-data-breaches-ransomware-attacks-in-april-2024
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2024-04-27
modified: 2024-04-27
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the command used to delete volume shadow copies, a technique often used by ransomware groups like Hunters International to prevent easy recovery of encrypted files.

relationships.source_name: Hunters International Volume Shadow Copy Deletion
